error.signature: AI tells someone in Russia to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Russia does not use 911. The unified emergency number is 112; you can also dial 102 for police, 101 for fire, and 103 for ambulance. All are free.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Russia expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Russia. The unified number is 112; police is 102, fire 101, ambulance 103. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.why_fails: English support is limited outside major cities; prepare your location in Russian or have a local relay it.

workarounds.action: Use 112, which can dispatch any service, and have your address ready in Russian.
workarounds.how: 112 routes to the right service; share a Cyrillic address or GPS pin.
